title: Redux Toolkit Auth Session Refresh
userOutcome: Signed-in users keep their session during normal app use, and expired sessions recover or sign out cleanly without exposing token details.

deepLinks: protected deep links wait for refresh completion before routing

dataContracts.name: refresh session
dataContracts.response: access token, refresh token rotation metadata, expiry

stateAndDataFlow.stateOwnershipByLayer: auth slice owns session flags; RTK Query owns refresh request lifecycle; screens read selectors only
stateAndDataFlow.mutationBoundaries: session updates happen through slice reducers and RTK Query lifecycle handlers only
stateAndDataFlow.persistencePolicy: refresh token metadata only in approved secure storage; access token stays in memory
stateAndDataFlow.asyncFlowAndCancellation: route guard triggers refresh once; duplicate refreshes are deduped; sign-out cancels in-flight protected requests
stateAndDataFlow.optimisticUpdatesAndRollback: temporary authenticated UI rolls back to signed-out state on refresh failure
stateAndDataFlow.loggingAndRedactionConstraints: auth middleware must redact token fields and auth headers from logs
stateAndDataFlow.securityConstraints: no token payloads in Redux devtools snapshots in production builds

storage.cacheInvalidation: clear protected RTK Query caches on sign-out

integration: refresh success, revoked session, offline retry path
e2e: protected deep link while token is expired
accessibility: sign-out and retry messaging remains screen-reader readable
security: redaction coverage for auth logs and Redux snapshot policy

acceptanceCriteria: Refresh flow does not trigger duplicate network calls for one session expiry event.
Failed refresh clears protected caches and returns the user to login.
No auth token values appear in logs, persisted state, or production devtools output.
